commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Paul says that a Gentile can be sure that he is justified by faith without doing the works of the law, e. g., circumcision or new moons or the veneration of the sabbath. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: This must not be understood in such a way as to say that a man who has received faith and continues to live is righteous, even though he leads a wicked life.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Paul does not say a “Jew” or “one under the law” but widens the discussion and opens the doors of faith to the world, saying a “man,” i.e., the name common to our race.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
